concerns.text: Task T012 (Orchestration) is listed AFTER T014 (Mapping) and T015 (Merge). T012 explicitly calls T014 and T015. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule; the orchestration task cannot execute the sub-tasks it depends on if they are not yet implemented. T012 must precede T014/T015 or be split into a 'Run' task that comes after implementation.

Task T020 (Compute Spearman) is marked as incomplete/missing, yet T021 (Regression) and T023 (FDR) depend on its output (`data/processed/correlation_results.csv`). The plan implies T020 produces the intermediate artifact required for T021/T023. If T020 is not implemented, the downstream tasks have no input data, breaking the dependency chain.

Task T026 (Heatmap) and T028 (Report) depend on `data/processed/analysis_results.csv` (output of T024). T024 is marked complete, but T020 (the source of the correlation data feeding T024) is incomplete. The chain T020 -> T024 -> T026/T028 is broken at the root. T020 must be prioritized and completed before T024 can be considered valid.

Task T008 (Synthetic Data) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T012 (Orchestration) depends on T008 to generate the fallback dataset. If T008 is run in parallel with T012, T012 may attempt to read `data/processed/synthetic_data.csv` before T008 finishes writing it. T008 should be ordered before T012, or T012 must explicitly invoke T008 synchronously (which contradicts the [P] tag if T008 is treated as a separate parallel task).

Task T010 and T011 (Tests) are listed before T014-T017 (Implementation). While the 'Tests First' methodology is correct, the [P] tag on T010/T011 implies they can run in parallel with implementation. Tests for US1 cannot run until the code (T014-T017) exists. The [P] tag is misleading here; these tests are dependent on the implementation tasks, not parallel to them.
